title: The Algorithmic Grimoire
author: Nia Byte
description: A junior mage discovers her spellbook is actually a machine-learning model with opinions, bugs, and one extremely judgmental raven.

title: Clean Code for Chaotic Goblins
author: Mara Stack
description: A practical guide to turning cursed code caves into maintainable systems without losing the goblin sparkle.

title: Left Pad at the End of the Universe
author: Ursula Kern
description: A starship crew must restore a missing dependency before the galaxy renders slightly off-centre forever.

title: Zero Trust Tea Ceremony
author: Akira Shell
description: A cyber-detective infiltrates a luxury tea syndicate where every kettle has an identity provider and every biscuit is suspicious.

title: Refactoring the Dragon
author: Nia Byte
description: A village architect learns the dragon is not evil, just badly coupled to a legacy treasure-hoarding workflow.

title: Architecture Patterns for Bookish Robots
author: Diego Claw
description: A sharp, cheeky field guide to modular storefronts, search services, carts, catalogues, and the robots who love them.

title: The Kubernetes Coven
author: Priya Pods
description: Three platform witches tame a haunted cluster before the full moon autoscaler turns the village into YAML.

title: The Last Commit Before Dawn
author: Jon Merge
description: A release manager has one night to find the rogue commit before production wakes up and chooses violence.

title: Prompt Engineering for Time Travellers
author: Elena Turing-Wells
description: A practical and paradox-safe guide to prompting models across timelines, stakeholders, and awkward steering committees.

title: Data Lakes and Other Mythical Swamps
author: Samir Schema
description: A lovingly grumpy tour through data strategy, governance, lineage, and why your lake keeps becoming soup.

title: Neon Samurai Debugger
author: Akira Shell
description: A wandering debugger cuts through corrupted city firmware with a blade made of logs, traces, and righteous indentation.

title: Microservices for Introverts
author: Lena Queue
description: A gentle guide to distributed systems for people who would rather the services talk to each other so they do not have to.
